summary_one_line: An attribute whose full name equals the literal string 'xml:id' must appear in the parsed XML input.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must carry an attribute fullname byte-equal to the 6-byte literal "xml:id" so that xmlStrEqual(fullname, BAD_CAST "xml:id") returns true at SAX2.c:1415.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winning vpc seeds embed the literal 'xml:' prefix bytes (78 6d 6c 3a) inside a doctype/element region and vpc's I2SRandReplace can splice the logged 'xml:id' comparand operand directly onto the input bytes feeding fullname, producing the exact match; lineage shows an I2S-floor (mutation_op = -) ancestor.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; its loser seeds carry unrelated attribute names like 'xlink:type' and never assemble the exact 6-byte 'xml:id' fullname, so the branch stays on the false side (resolved=0/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace logs the constant comparand 'xml:id' from the xmlStrEqual CMP and substitutes it onto matching input bytes, instantly satisfying the literal equality that value_profile's havoc/CMP_MAP-only stack cannot reliably construct byte-by-byte.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is an exact string-equality test against the literal "xml:id".
The winner value_profile_cmplog resolves the true side in all trials while loser value_profile never does.
The loser value_profile is blocked in every trial.
Winning seeds embed the literal 'xml:' prefix bytes feeding the fullname comparison.
Loser seeds carry unrelated attribute names (xlink:type) rather than 'xml:id'.
value_profile_cmplog provides the I2SRandReplace input-to-state mutator that the loser lacks.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6233 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed c20581314eb72916` and observed an I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 6 of the 7-level chain, the dash-row floor signal for an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on xmlStrEqual against a literal 'xml:id' in which value_profile (no I2S) resolves the true branch as often as value_profile_cmplog, showing the exact-literal gate is solvable without I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The winning seeds show 'xml:' bytes but the visible hex windows do not display a fully contiguous 'xml:id' fullname, so the exact-match assembly is inferred from source plus the lineage dash row rather than directly read off seed bytes.
